title: Semantic search idea
content: Build a note search prototype where a query about meaning can find notes even when the exact words are different. Use embeddings, cosine similarity, and a compact Streamlit interface.

title: TF-IDF baseline
content: Create a simple keyword search baseline with TF-IDF. It should be easy to compare against semantic search and explain in the methodology chapter.

title: Embedding model choice
content: The all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model is small enough for a research demo and produces sentence embeddings suitable for personal notes.

title: PKM research hypothesis
content: Hypothesis: semantic search based on embeddings returns more relevant personal notes than keyword matching when users search with vague or indirect wording.

title: Automatic tagging plan
content: Represent each predefined tag as an embedding and compare note embeddings with tag embeddings. Select top tags by cosine similarity.

title: Category descriptions
content: Use short category descriptions instead of category names only. Descriptions give the embedding model more semantic context.

title: Research metrics
content: Evaluate retrieval quality with Precision at K, Recall at K, nDCG at K, and Mean Reciprocal Rank.

title: Experiment queries
content: Prepare a small set of realistic information needs, such as finding database indexing notes, AI summarization ideas, and productivity workflows.

title: Manual relevance labels
content: For each query, mark relevant note ids manually. The labels will make the comparison between TF-IDF and semantic search reproducible.

title: Streamlit UI sketch
content: The interface needs a note list, selected note preview, search box, search mode switch, results, automatic tags, category, and similar notes.

title: Python environment note
content: Use Python 3.11 or newer with pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, sentence-transformers, Streamlit, matplotlib, and plotly.

title: Vector similarity
content: Cosine similarity works well when embeddings are normalized. Ranking can be implemented with numpy argsort for a small dataset.

title: Database index analogy
content: Vector search can be explained as an index over meanings, while TF-IDF is an index over words and phrases.

title: SQLite storage idea
content: A later prototype might store notes in SQLite with embeddings serialized as arrays, but CSV is enough for the first experiment.

title: Obsidian import idea
content: Future work could parse markdown files from an Obsidian vault and convert them into the same notes dataframe.

title: Note summarization idea
content: Add automatic note summaries after the search prototype is stable. Summaries could support faster result inspection.

title: Personal dashboard note
content: A PKM dashboard should help the user review recent ideas, unfinished tasks, and related concepts without becoming a full project management tool.

title: Learning journal
content: Keep short learning notes after each research session: what was tested, what failed, what should be tried next.

title: Backend scope decision
content: Avoid a full backend for the research prototype. Streamlit can run the demo and keep the architecture readable.

title: Frontend minimalism
content: The UI should be calm and functional. The value is in the search behavior and experiment results, not in complex visual design.

title: Machine learning tags
content: Useful tags include machine learning, embeddings, ai, research, productivity, python, databases, backend, frontend, and architecture.

title: Knowledge graph idea
content: A later extension could visualize note relationships as a graph, using semantic similarity as edge weight.

title: Related notes feature
content: Similar notes can be found by comparing the selected note embedding to all other note embeddings.

title: Keyword weakness example
content: A note about vector representations may be relevant to a query about semantic memory even if it never says the word memory.

title: Semantic strength example
content: Embedding search should retrieve notes about categorization when the query says automatic organization of personal knowledge.

title: Research chapter outline
content: Chapter structure: problem statement, related work, system design, prototype implementation, experiments, results, and limitations.

title: Limitations note
content: The dataset is synthetic and small, so conclusions should be framed as prototype-level evidence rather than universal proof.

title: Precision at K
content: Precision at K measures how many of the top K retrieved notes are relevant. It is simple and easy to explain.

title: Recall at K
content: Recall at K measures how many known relevant notes are found in the top K results.

title: nDCG explanation
content: nDCG rewards systems that place highly relevant documents closer to the top of the result list.

title: MRR explanation
content: Mean Reciprocal Rank focuses on the position of the first relevant result and is useful for search tasks.

title: Research reproducibility
content: Keep query sets, relevance labels, code, and generated result tables in the experiments directory.

title: Embeddings cache idea
content: For a larger dataset, cache note embeddings to disk to avoid recomputing them every Streamlit run.

title: FAISS optional
content: FAISS is useful for fast vector search on large collections, but numpy similarity is enough for 50 to 150 notes.

title: Data cleaning
content: Normalize empty fields, combine title and content, and keep note ids stable so relevance labels remain valid.

title: Productivity workflow
content: Use weekly reviews to connect raw notes with projects. Search should help rediscover old insights.

title: Task capture
content: A personal knowledge tool should capture tasks without forcing every thought into a rigid database schema.

title: Project planning
content: Break the prototype into dataset, embedding pipeline, keyword baseline, UI, auto-tagging, categorization, and evaluation.

title: API design note
content: Keep search modules independent from Streamlit so experiments can reuse the same code without UI dependencies.

title: Testing idea
content: Add smoke tests for dataset loading, search result shape, and tagging output before expanding the experiment scripts.

title: Research paper notes
content: Look for papers about semantic information retrieval, dense passage retrieval, and personal information management.

title: Embedding visualization
content: Use PCA or UMAP later to project note embeddings into two dimensions and show clusters by category.

title: Plotly chart idea
content: A comparison page can plot Precision at K for TF-IDF and semantic search side by side.

title: Backend architecture
content: If the prototype grows, separate data loading, indexing, ranking, tagging, and evaluation modules.

title: Database schema thought
content: A simple notes table needs id, title, content, created date, source, tags, category, and embedding reference.

title: Python notebook experiment
content: Use notebooks for exploratory checks, but move reusable search and metric code into application modules.

title: AI categorization
content: Automatic categorization can use nearest category description embeddings. It is transparent enough for a thesis prototype.

title: Frontend note browser
content: A note browser should make selection simple: sidebar list, main preview, and compact metadata display.

title: Search comparison screenshot
content: For the thesis, capture a query where semantic search finds conceptually related notes that keyword search misses.

title: Personal reflection
content: The system should feel like a thinking aid: it helps reconnect scattered notes, not replace the user's judgment.

title: Research risks
content: Main risks are small dataset bias, weak relevance labels, and overclaiming experimental results.

title: Implementation milestone
content: First milestone is a working local demo. Second milestone is labeled queries and metric tables.

title: Semantic query example
content: Query: how can I automatically organize my notes. Expected results should include tagging, categories, and PKM workflow notes.

title: Keyword query example
content: Query: TF-IDF baseline should retrieve notes that explicitly mention TF-IDF and keyword search.

title: Database query example
content: Query: storage for embeddings should surface SQLite storage, database schema, and vector index notes.

title: Architecture query example
content: Query: keep modules reusable should find API design, backend architecture, and project planning notes.

title: Productivity query example
content: Query: weekly review and task capture should find productivity workflow and personal dashboard notes.

title: AI query example
content: Query: sentence model for semantic retrieval should find embedding model choice and vector similarity notes.

title: Research evaluation query
content: Query: compare retrieval methods should find research metrics, experiment queries, and manual relevance labels.

title: Future extension
content: Future extensions include markdown import, embedding cache, relevance feedback, graph visualization, and summary generation.
